survey_title: Learning from Mistakes Attitude & Academic Growth Tracking Scale

questions.text: When you make an academic mistake, how do you typically feel afterward?
How often do you reflect on the reasons behind your academic errors?
When you receive a low grade, what is your first reaction?

options.text: Analyse what went wrong and plan to fix it
Feel frustrated and dwell on it

questions.text: How frequently do you revisit past mistakes to extract lessons from them?
Do you believe your academic abilities can be developed through effort?
How often do you actively seek constructive feedback after making errors?
When you struggle with an academic topic, how do you respond?

options.text: Seek extra resources and help immediately
Struggle on my own for a long time
Avoid the topic as much as possible

questions.text: How often do you set new academic goals after identifying a personal weakness?
How comfortable are you discussing your academic mistakes with classmates or teachers?
How often do you keep a record tracking your academic progress and improvements?
When you fail a test, how quickly do you get back on track?

options.text: Immediately — I start planning my recovery

questions.text: Do you view constructive criticism as an opportunity to grow?
How often do you compare your current academic performance to your past performance?

options.text: Always — I track my progress carefully

questions.text: When classmates outperform you, how does it make you feel?
How often do you revise your study strategies after poor academic results?
How strongly do you believe that mistakes are a natural and essential part of learning?
How often do you try a different approach after failing at an academic task?
How do you handle repeated mistakes in the same subject area?

options.text: Seek specific guidance from a teacher
Try harder without changing my approach

questions.text: How often do you acknowledge and celebrate your own academic growth over time?
When given a chance to redo or correct an assignment, how do you approach it?

options.text: I thoroughly analyse errors and resubmit with full effort
I correct errors and make some improvements
I make minimal changes just to pass

psychological_states.label: Exceptional Growth Mindset
psychological_states.description: You have an outstanding attitude toward mistakes and excellent academic resilience. You consistently turn errors into growth opportunities. No intervention needed — keep inspiring others!

psychological_states.label: Strong Positive Attitude
psychological_states.description: You embrace mistakes as learning opportunities and actively track your academic growth. Your mindset is healthy and productive. Continue applying your reflective habits.

psychological_states.label: Good Tracking Ability
psychological_states.description: You generally learn from mistakes but could improve consistency in reflection. Consider keeping a more structured error journal to sustain your growth trajectory.

psychological_states.label: Moderate Growth Orientation
psychological_states.description: You sometimes struggle with processing mistakes constructively. Try reframing errors as data points rather than failures. Practising self-compassion and structured reflection may help.

psychological_states.label: Low Growth Orientation
psychological_states.description: You tend to avoid mistakes rather than learn from them. Keeping an error journal and seeking regular feedback from teachers is strongly recommended to build a healthier academic mindset.

psychological_states.label: Fixed Mindset Tendency
psychological_states.description: Mistakes significantly affect your academic confidence and motivation. Seeking academic counselling or support resources is recommended. Remember: every expert was once a beginner who did not give up.
